title: dianthus crassipes subsp. serenaeus
intro: Dianthus crassipes subsp. serenaeus, commonly known as the serenade pink or serenae pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Dianthus crassipes subsp. serenaeus are said to include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating menstrual cramps, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and infusions-based remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
